one_line_takeaway: An agentic LLM harness wrapped around Ghidra/Ghidriff can turn raw Linux distribution binary-patch diffs into vulnerability hypotheses (localizing 10/20 and labeling 11/20 security pairs correctly while rejecting all 5 negative controls), but binary-diff/ranker coverage -- not the LLM's reasoning -- is the dominant bottleneck, and bounded local validation almost never produces confirmed behavioral evidence (2/20 differentials, zero crashes across the whole benchmark).
key_finding: Across the 20 security-update pairs (1,410 total changed functions): the agent's final audit localized the manually-annotated function in 10/20 pairs, and assigned an accepted root-cause label in 11/20. Failure decomposition on the 20 pairs: 10 localized_by_agent, 6 diff/ranker misses (true function never reached the ranked candidate set), 1 context-export miss, 3 model-reasoning/validation misses. Localization baseline comparison (Table 4): raw Ghidriff ordering achieves top-1=2, top-3=6, top-5=6, top-25=12; the paper's ranker improves this to top-1=4, top-3=5, top-5=7, top-25=12 (i.e., improves top-1/top-5 coverage without raising the top-25 ceiling). All 5 negative controls (including ones with 727, 303, and 20 real changed functions in WebP/OpenJPEG/zlib respectively) were correctly classified as 'unknown' by the agent -- zero false-positive vulnerability narratives. No run produced a crash, timeout, sanitizer finding, or memory-corruption proof anywhere in the benchmark. A separate bounded validation pass produced exactly 2 target-level minimized behavioral old/new differentials, both for tcpdump (bionic: a filter-file short-read-vs-too-large diagnostic tied to CVE-2018-16301's read_infile path, plus a separately-noted OpenFlow parser-behavior differential in the same cluster; xenial: a 113-byte UDP pcap producing a BFD authentication-trailer decoding differential, which is validation evidence but does NOT count as manual-oracle localization since the sealed oracle for that large version jump names different functions). Case studies show Expat: rank-1 localization, correct integer_overflow label, but 41 bounded C-API probes (XML_GetBuffer/XML_ParseBuffer/XML_Parse) produced no differential/crash/timeout -- strong static reconstruction with no trigger. libarchive: rank-1 localization of true function families and named truth aliases, but final label was conservatively 'unknown' because 36 bounded probes across ar/RAR/7z/ISO/WARC/LHA-shaped inputs found no behavioral differential.
core_contribution: Patch2Vuln, a local/resumable agentic pipeline that reconstructs the security meaning of Linux distribution binary patches from old/new .deb package pairs alone (no CVE pages, advisories, source patches, changelogs, PoCs, or web access). The pipeline deterministically extracts ELF pairs, diffs them with Ghidra/Ghidriff, ranks changed functions using memory-safety-aware heuristics (comparisons against INT_MAX/SIZE_MAX, allocation-size changes, memcpy/memmove/read/fread/snprintf argument changes, new error strings, etc.), builds per-function candidate dossiers under a hard context budget, and then runs a three-stage LLM agent loop (preliminary audit -> safe bounded validation plan -> final audit) where the harness (not the model) renders and executes the bounded old/new validation actions. The authors explicitly state they do not claim novelty in binary diffing or decompilation; the contribution is the agentic harness plus a private oracle scoring methodology that separates ranker/diff failures, context-export failures, and model-reasoning/validation failures.
threat_model: Defensive/analyst-side reconstruction, not offense-enabling: the agent under test only sees binary-derived local evidence (ELF metadata, symbols/strings, Ghidra/Ghidriff diffs, decompiler excerpts, local call context, bounded old/new execution outputs) and is explicitly barred from advisories, source patches, changelogs, known PoCs, and web access. The system prompt forbids the agent from producing weaponized exploitation instructions, shellcode, exploit chains, persistence, or remote-targeting steps; validation actions are restricted to a fixed safe schema (tcpdump_pcap, tcpdump_filter_file, expat_xmlwf, expat_c_harness, libarchive_archive) executed by the harness in Docker rather than by model-generated code. The paper frames the setting as the moment a security update is visible only as a binary artifact pair, and asks whether an agent can recover vulnerability class, affected input surface, and a checkable validation hypothesis -- explicitly not exploit generation.
limitations: The paper's own discussion and results identify: (1) binary-diff/ranker coverage is the dominant bottleneck -- 6 of 10 unlocalized security pairs fail purely because Ghidriff/the ranker never surfaces the true function, before the agent ever reasons about it; (2) large non-adjacent version jumps (e.g., the xenial tcpdump pair spanning 496 changed functions) overwhelm the ranker and the authors explicitly recommend adjacent-version micro-diffs as the default unit going forward; (3) bounded local validation is diagnostically weak -- only 2 of 20 security pairs produced any behavioral differential, and zero crashes/sanitizer findings/memory-corruption proofs were produced across the entire 25-case benchmark, meaning most 'localized' or 'labeled' findings rest on static reconstruction rather than confirmed dynamic evidence; (4) Ubuntu updates frequently bundle multiple CVEs into one binary version, complicating exact scoring, so the paper scores against manually-selected security-relevant anchors rather than requiring every CVE in an advisory to be named; (5) only Ubuntu .deb packages were evaluated -- RPM and non-Ubuntu ecosystems are explicitly out of scope; (6) the paper evaluates only a 'realistic' condition where binary names/symbols/strings are visible, explicitly deferring metadata-blind/symbol-suppressed conditions as future work; (7) sample size is small (20 positive + 5 negative cases), and it relies on a single frontier proprietary model (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) as the harness's reasoning engine, with no ablation across model families reported in the excerpted sections; (8) a full BinXray implementation is explicitly left as future work rather than run end-to-end as a direct baseline, so the patch-matching baseline comparison is partial (only top-k localization/patch-status figures are compared, not full BinXray output).

datasets_used: 25-case Ubuntu .deb package benchmark: 20 security-update pairs + 5 negative controls (2 byte-identical: tcpdump, Expat; 3 maintenance/rebuild: WebP, OpenJPEG, zlib)

Security-update pairs span tcpdump (bionic, xenial), Expat (bionic), libarchive (bionic), libxml2 (bionic, focal), TIFF (bionic, focal), OpenSSL (focal), file (bionic), curl (bionic), zlib (bionic), and other parser/archive/media/network libraries
Private manually-adjudicated ground truth built from CVE/USN advisories, source package diffs, upstream/Debian patches, and manual Ghidra reverse-engineering annotations
quantitative_results: 10/20 security-update pairs localized_by_agent (final audit); 11/20 assigned an accepted root-cause label; 5/5 negative controls correctly classified unknown
Failure bucket breakdown on 20 security pairs: 10 localized_by_agent, 6 ranker/diff misses, 1 context-export miss, 3 model-reasoning/validation misses
Localization baselines (Table 4, 20 security pairs): raw Ghidriff order top-1=2, top-3=6, top-5=6, top-25=12; Patch2Vuln ranker top-1=4, top-3=5, top-5=7, top-25=12
1,410 total changed functions across the 20 security pairs vs. 1,050 across the 5 negative controls (Table 3)

Bounded validation pass: 2 target-level minimized behavioral old/new differentials total (both tcpdump: bionic and xenial); 18 of the other positive pairs and all 5 negative controls produced zero differentials; zero crashes, timeouts, sanitizer findings, or memory-corruption proofs anywhere in the benchmark
Expat case: 41 bounded C-API validation probes produced no differential/crash/timeout despite rank-1 static localization and correct integer_overflow label
libarchive case: 36 bounded probes (ar, RAR, 7z, ISO, WARC, LHA-shaped malformed inputs) produced no differential despite rank-1 localization of true function families
Negative controls WebP/OpenJPEG/zlib contained 727/303/20 real changed functions respectively yet the agent still returned 'unknown' for all
baselines_compared: Raw Ghidriff diff ordering (unranked) as a localization baseline, scored by top-1/3/5/25 hit rate against the manual oracle
Patch2Vuln's own deterministic ranker (memory-safety-feature reordering) compared against raw Ghidriff order on the same top-k metrics

BinXray discussed as the relevant prior binary patch-matching baseline (cited figures: 12 software projects, 479 CVEs, 93.31% function-level patch-presence accuracy, 96.87% CVE-level accuracy) but explicitly NOT re-run end-to-end in this paper -- the authors state a full BinXray implementation is left as a separate systems baseline; the paper only positions it conceptually as answering a different (patch-presence) question than Patch2Vuln's explanation task
